task_completion.description: Load the sequence of FITS images from the time_series directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image processing application (like AstroImageJ or ImageJ) is open, showing an image loaded from the '~/AstroImages/time_series/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the loaded image stack contains exactly 30 frames
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image window displays a stack slider or header text indicating exactly 30 frames or slices (e.g., '1/30' or '30 slices').

task_completion.description: Adjust display brightness and contrast to make the star field clearly visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Brightness/Contrast (B&C) adjustment window is visible, and the main image display shows distinct, clear stars against a darker background rather than being washed out or completely black.

task_completion.description: Set the export video frame rate to exactly 10 frames per second
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export as AVI' or 'Save as AVI' dialog box is open with the frame rate (FPS) input field explicitly set to '10'.

task_completion.description: Save the animation as tracking_video.avi in the AstroImages directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save file dialog or file manager shows the file being saved with the exact name 'tracking_video.avi' in the '/home/ga/AstroImages/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the application's graphical interface to process the images
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the AstroImageJ/ImageJ GUI menus (e.g., File -> Import -> Image Sequence, File -> Save As -> AVI) rather than just executing a terminal command like ffmpeg.

integrity.description: Verify the brightness and contrast were actually adjusted visually
integrity.visual_evidence: The image window should show a noticeable visual improvement in star visibility compared to the raw, unadjusted FITS file, confirming the B&C tool was genuinely used.

integrity.description: Verify the AVI file is exported properly through the software
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog should belong to the image processing application's AVI export feature, not just renaming a file extension or creating a dummy file in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/AstroImages/tracking_video.avi'. The loaded image sequence must contain exactly 30 frames. The export frame rate must be exactly 10.0 FPS. The data source is the WASP-12b Calibrated Images dataset.
